Title: Ubiquitination of phytoene synthase 1 precursor modulates carotenoid biosynthesis in tomato

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol and nucleus; colocalizes with PPSR1
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit nutritional quality and pigmentation; vitamin A precursor and antioxidant source
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with PPSR1 in the ubiquitination machinery that negatively regulates carotenoid biosynthesis through PSY1 precursor degradation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; in vitro pull-down; co-IP; in vitro ubiquitination assay using recombinant SlUBC32
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132 treatment in co-IP assays; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in a screen for SlUBC32-interacting proteins. Although important mechanistically, it was not the main causal gene manipulated for the carotenoid phenotype in tomato fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlUBC32 directly interacts with PPSR1 in yeast, tobacco, and in vitro. 2. Recombinant SlUBC32 supports PPSR1 ubiquitination activity in vitro. 3. It forms part of the SlUBC32-PPSR1-PSY1 module affecting carotenoid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary engineering target in ubiquitin-mediated control of plastid precursor stability, but direct carotenoid trait validation in this paper centers on PPSR1.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phytoene synthase; plastid-targeted carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme with N-terminal chloroplast transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core carotenoid pathway; entry step to carotenogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: First committed step; upstream; rate-limiting entry point
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to fruit color and nutritional quality; provides vitamin A precursors and antioxidant benefits; functions in plants as photosynthetic components and precursors of ABA and strigolactones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PSY1 protein abundance increases phytoene, lycopene, and β-carotene accumulation in ripening tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as the main rate-limiting enzyme and first committed step in carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the first committed carotenoid intermediate and controls flux toward carotenoid end products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PSY1 is the only PSY protein associated with carotenoid biosynthesis during tomato fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mature protein in plastid; precursor detected in cytosol and interacts with PPSR1 there
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ proteomics, PRIDE PXD018731; ubiquitinated peptide SWATH-MS / ubiquitin-remnant proteomics, PRIDE PXD018707
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of PPSR1 followed by PSY1 protein accumulation analysis; ubiquitin-remnant MS identifying Lys380 and Lys406; Y2H; LCI; pull-down; co-IP; in vivo ubiquitination assay; CHX degradation assays with lysine-to-arginine mutants; immunoblot; carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stages 34/38/41/45 DPA; MG132 proteasome inhibitor; cycloheximide treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increase fruit carotenoid content by preventing PSY1 precursor degradation, e.g. edit PPSR1 or modify PSY1 ubiquitination sites Lys380/Lys406 while monitoring broader ripening effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two ubiquitination sites identified at Lys380 and Lys406. PPSR1 binds the PSY1 precursor rather than mature plastid-localized PSY1. PSY1 transcript level was unchanged in ppsr1 mutants while protein increased ~3-fold, indicating post-translational regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY1 was identified as a candidate PPSR1 substrate by quantitative proteomics and ubiquitin-remnant profiling. 2. PPSR1 directly interacts with the PSY1 precursor in the cytosol and ubiquitinates it. 3. Ubiquitination at Lys380 and Lys406 promotes PSY1 degradation via the 26S proteasome. 4. Elevated PSY1 protein in ppsr1 mutants increases carotenoid accumulation during fruit ripening.

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol and nucleus; colocalizes with PSY1 precursor in cytosol
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves fruit color and nutritional quality; source of vitamin A precursors and antioxidants
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational control of carotenoid biosynthesis through precursor protein degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ubiquitin-mediated proteolytic regulation; promotes ubiquitination and 26S proteasomal degradation of PSY1 precursor
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively controls PSY1 protein abundance by promoting precursor degradation; thereby restricts carotenoid biosynthetic flux
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with E2 enzyme SlUBC32; PPSR1 self-interacts/dimerizes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit developmental/ripening stage; exact upstream regulators not established
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible self-regulation via self-ubiquitination; speculative feedback through PPSR1-targeted transcriptional regulators mentioned but not established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PPSR1 function accelerates fruit coloration and increases phytoene, lycopene, and β-carotene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally decisive negative regulator of carotenoid accumulation in ripening tomato fruit by controlling abundance of the key enzyme PSY1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, and fruits; transcript declines during fruit ripening while protein remains relatively stable
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High enough in ripening fruit to modulate carotenoid accumulation; transcript declines during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H screen and validation; LCI; in vitro pull-down; co-IP; in vitro self-ubiquitination assay; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; cell-free degradation assay; transient expression; in vivo ubiquitination assay; proteomics; carotenoid quantification
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ proteomics, PRIDE PXD018731; ubiquitinated peptide SWATH-MS / ubiquitin-remnant proteomics, PRIDE PXD018707
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native gene in tomato mutants; 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression assays; CRISPR sgRNA cassettes driven by AtU3b and AtU3d in editing construct; Cas9 in pYLCRISPR/Cas9Pubi-H
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stages (34-45 DPA); MG132 proteasome inhibitor; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ppsr1 mutants display ripening-accelerated phenotype; PPSR1 likely affects additional proteins beyond PSY1, potentially influencing other fruit quality traits such as ethylene and aroma volatile pathways
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target for biofortification: knocking out or weakening PPSR1 can elevate fruit carotenoids. Deployment should consider pleiotropic effects on ripening and other PPSR1 substrates
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PPSR1 has self-ubiquitination activity, requires intact RING residues C49/H51/H54, and is degraded via the 26S proteasome. It appears to target plastid-destined precursor proteins in the cytosol and acts independently of HSP70/HSP90 in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPSR1 is a tomato RING E3 ubiquitin ligase that interacts with SlUBC32 and self-ubiquitinates. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 loss-of-function mutants accumulate more carotenoids and show earlier fruit coloration. 3. PPSR1 directly binds the PSY1 precursor and ubiquitinates it for proteasomal degradation. 4. By lowering PSY1 steady-state protein level, PPSR1 negatively modulates carotenoid biosynthesis during fruit ripening.
